mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate directly boosts corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-GDP-growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating federal funds rate hikes compress equity valuations by raising the discount rate for future cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the rate acceleration signal, or forward returns show no statistical difference from non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong nonfarm payroll growth and a declining unemployment rate signal a robust labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rebound in the industrial production index following a contraction indicates restored manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive mean difference during INDPRO rebound episodes compared to flat or declining periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in housing starts below the 1200 thousand unit threshold signals weakening aggregate demand and construction sector distress, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where housing starts breach the 1200 threshold, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month increases in the consumer price index erode real purchasing power and trigger inflation fears, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no negative correlation with CPI acceleration, or the signal produces positive returns in over 60% of high-inflation months.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth slows while industrial production remains elevated indicates a supply-demand mismatch that pressures corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from periods of aligned GDP and production trends.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls during the initial phase of an economic cycle signal strong labor demand and wage growth, fueling consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive mean during high-payroll-growth months in early-cycle phases, or the predictive power vanishes after controlling for baseline market trends.
